CVE-2025-23395.title: Screen 5.0.0 when it runs with setuid-root privileges does not dr ...
CVE-2025-23395.summary: Screen 5.0.0 when it runs with setuid-root privileges does not drop privileges while operating on a user supplied path. This allows unprivileged users to create files in arbitrary locations with `root` ownership, the invoking user's (real) group ownership and file mode 0644. All data written to the Screen PTY will be logged into this file, allowing to escalate to root privileges

CVE-2025-46802.title: For a short time they PTY is set to mode 666, allowing any user o ...
CVE-2025-46802.summary: For a short time they PTY is set to mode 666, allowing any user on the system to connect to the screen session.

CVE-2025-46803.title: The default mode of pseudo terminals (PTYs) allocated by Screen w ...
CVE-2025-46803.summary: The default mode of pseudo terminals (PTYs) allocated by Screen was changed from 0620 to 0622, thereby allowing anyone to write to any Screen PTYs in the system.

CVE-2025-46804.title: A minor information leak when running Screen with setuid-root pri ...
CVE-2025-46804.summary:
A minor information leak when running Screen with setuid-root privileges allows unprivileged users to deduce information about a path that would otherwise not be available.


Affected are older Screen versions, as well as version 5.0.0.

CVE-2025-46805.title: Screen version 5.0.0 and older version 4 releases have  a TOCTOU  ...
CVE-2025-46805.summary: Screen version 5.0.0 and older version 4 releases have  a TOCTOU race potentially allowing to send SIGHUP, SIGCONT to privileged processes when installed setuid-root.
